page.content: In the Environmental Compliance Fines Report for Covanta Holdings, covering the period of January 2024, the following data is presented: under the rating "A," the status of "Open" pertains to the category "AirQuality" with a fine amount of $50,000, while the category "WasteManagement" also rated "A" has a fine of $75,000. The category "Recycling" under status "Closed" has a fine of $10,000; "HazardousWaste" is similarly closed with a fine of $30,000. The "ErosionControl" category is open and fined $15,000, whereas "Landfill," which is closed, has a fine of $5,000. The "SoilContamination" category is open with a fine of $60,000, while "NoiseViolation" is closed at $8,000. The "OdorComplaint" category incurs an open fine of $12,000, while "Stormwater," closed, has a fine of $18,000.

page.content: The status of "Open" continues with "PermitViolation" at $22,000, while "Spillage," closed, amounts to $9,000. For "IllegalDumping," the fine is $40,000, and "EmissionStandards," closed, has a fine of $70,000. "WasteTracking" carries an open fine of $14,000, while "ComplianceAudit," closed, is fined $11,000. The "EnvironmentalImpact" category, rated "A" and open, has a fine of $55,000, while "RegulatoryReporting," closed, amounts to $19,000. Finally, "WasteReduction" is open with a fine of $21,000, and "ResourceRecovery," closed, has a fine of $17,000. The category "Pending" remains applicable for all entries not listed as closed.
